title: Gate new durable components with source-bound admission evidence
goal: Add a lightweight system-wide admission contract for newly introduced durable repositories, services, background processes and public operator surfaces. Each new durable component must name its purpose, concrete consumer, truth authority, admission class (replace, reduce, enable or experimental), operating dependencies and maintenance burden, and a review or retirement path; experimental components additionally require a success criterion and expiry. Existing components remain grandfathered and the contract must reuse Systemkatalog and Bureau truth instead of creating a new control plane or global complexity score.

approval.note: User explicitly authorized implementation on 2026-07-23. Preserve Systemkatalog as stable semantic authority and Bureau as task, priority and closeout authority; do not retroactively block existing components.

acceptance.assertion: A machine-readable contract covers newly introduced durable repositories, services, background processes and public operator surfaces and requires purpose, concrete consumer, truth authority, admission class, operating dependencies or maintenance burden and review or retirement path.
Admission class is exactly one of replace, reduce, enable or experimental; in experimental entries additionally require a measurable success criterion and expiry or review deadline.

The current durable component set is explicitly baselined or otherwise deterministically grandfathered so existing components are not retroactively blocked; newly introduced in-scope components without valid admission evidence fail validation.

The mechanism creates no runtime status, task, queue, dispatch, merge or deployment authority. Stable component semantics remain in Systemkatalog and implementation, priority and closeout remain in Bureau.
Systemkatalog validation consumes the admission contract, and Bureau task evidence can reference the source-bound admission record without duplicating the component registry.

Tests prove accepted replace, reduce or enable evidence, one accepted experimental case, rejection of missing consumer or authority, rejection of incomplete experimental evidence, and grandfathering of the pre-existing catalog.

does_not_establish: permission to mutate foreign dirty worktrees
requirement that every new component replace an old component

validation.system_catalog_validator: status valid; 35 in-scope durable components, all 35 grandfathered, 0 admitted additions at baseline.
validation.repository_contract: PASS; 139 tests with 1 expected skip; TARGET-PROOF SYSTEMKATALOG REPOSITORY CONTRACT VALID.
validation.post_merge_readback: The same full repository contract passed from a clean detached worktree at merge commit 7447da1f9b481d31fecd28a385ac1c3e27a22bbb.

live_proof.consumer: Leitstand is currently bound to the immutable Systemkatalog release rooted at the exact merge commit; ecosystem consumer readback reports 6/6 declared artifacts without manifest_corrupt.
live_proof.boundary: Component Admission is a repository validation and stable-semantics contract; it creates no separate runtime service or mutable authority.

verified_acceptance.admission-contract: Verified by policy/component-admission.v1.json, registry/ecosystem/component-admissions.v1.json and scripts/system_catalog_admissions.py merged in #146.
verified_acceptance.classes: Verified: replace, reduce, enable and experimental are the only admission classes; experimental requires successCriterion and non-expired reviewBy.
verified_acceptance.grandfathering: Verified: 35 existing durable components are SHA-256-bound as the grandfather baseline; silent baseline replacement or expansion fails closed.
verified_acceptance.authority-boundary: Verified: the contract creates no runtime, task, queue, dispatch, merge or deployment authority; Systemkatalog remains stable semantic authority and Bureau remains task/priority/closeout authority.
verified_acceptance.integration: Verified: validate_system_catalog.py consumes the admission validator and the source-bound registry remains in Systemkatalog without duplicating component truth in Bureau.
verified_acceptance.tests: Verified by focused 8/8 tests plus the full repository contract and post-merge clean-worktree validation.

metadata.verification_note: Systemkatalog PR #146 is merged at 7447da1f9b481d31fecd28a385ac1c3e27a22bbb; post-merge validators are green; the current immutable Systemkatalog consumer release is bound to that commit. Component Admission is therefore canonically implemented without introducing a second control plane.
